title: Hookera bivalvis
intro: Hookera bivalvis, also known as New Zealand hookera, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Hookera bivalvis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hookera bivalvis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to harness its therapeutic benefits.
